feed.title: SAP Community - Data and Analytics
feed.subtitle: Data and Analytics Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Wrong KPIs with SAP Marketing Cloud Landing Pages?
entry.content:
Hello SAP-community,

our customer created a form, embedded it in an landingpage, exported the HTML and published it with SAP Marketing Cloud. The exported html then was hosted externally.

After finishing multiple LinkedIn campaigns, aiming with all advertisments at the same URL (=the one of the hosted landingpage), the following effect was observed:

  • Now, comparing the KPIs of SMC and LinkedIn there is a high difference
  • (provided by SMC standard in Content Studio and LinkedIn Ads Manager) The KPIs of LinkedIn -> the clicks to the landingpage are 20x higher than the tracked views of the landing page.

Which effect are we missing here? It would be great to understand how SMC counts its views and other metrics for the forms/landingpages.

I thought that it may have something to do with the fact that the website is exported and hosted externally, but not sure yet. At least after publishing the landing page in SMC Content Studio, visiting it via the automatically generated SMC-URL and subscribing via the embedded form, works perfectly and is also counted correctly.

I will also update this post, if my tests are done.

I would be very thankful for your ideas!

Many thanks in advance,
Best regards, Stefan

entry.title: SAP IBP Analytic stories - Drill option in table and charts
entry.content:
Hi All,

In SAP IBP when we use the Manage Analytic stories to create a table or a chart I see the drill option grayed out in Tables and I do not see this option in Charts at all.

Can someone please explain the process to use the drill down option in Analytic stories in tables and charts like how we used to do in Advanced Analytics.

Warm Regards

entry.title: SAP Ariba: Export of Ariba Data - Data Warehouse (Data Strategy)
entry.content:
Hello ~ I am reaching out with this question to the SAP Ariba SCM community. We are using SAP Ariba Sourcing, Contracts, Buying, and Invoicing and are exploring options to export the data and land it in our data warehouse. This was attempted a couple of years ago with the creation of templates and exporting data via Analytical API. However, there are challenges with this approach: 1) Keeping Ariba record changes and deletions in synch with the DW; 2) Analytical API limits are reached due to high volumes of data; and 3) Templates that contain vector fields have a lower Analytical API limit (5,000 per day). Also, as an FYI, our ERP system is PeopleSoft Financials.
If you have successfully implemented an SAP Ariba data strategy with the export of data to a DW, I would really appreciate a response to this post. We are open to all ideas - internal middleware development using SAP Ariba APIs, SAP solutions, non-SAP solutions, etc. Thank you!

entry.title: How can i combine data in *new data model*ling approach in SAC? Sap analytics cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

in classic modelling approach in SAC which will be phased out sooner or later and there we wont use it anymore - you could glue your tables together using some dimensions (join, link, combine - call it what you want). I don't see a similar option in the "new data model" approach, if it's about 2 tables containing "facts", not talking about master data (aka dimension tables approach). 

Can pls someone help me? I come from QlikSense, where life is easy but not low-code/no-code. I'd like to give the low level SAC approach to a broad audience in our company, so that we get rid of now-excel processes.

Second Question: If i create a dimension table based on an XLS file on a file server, how can i update that dimension table? 

Thanks

entry.title: Repeat value from previous cell when it is blank per unique account
entry.content:
When creating a report, I want to have a repeat value from previous cell when it is blank per unique account. I already have a break on the account level but it is still not working. Below is the dummy data.

 

AccountCurrent ValueWanted Value
123ABCABC
123 ABC
456CDECDE
456 CDE
456 CDE
580FYIFYI
580 FYI

entry.title: Data conversion in the target system or in the source system
entry.content:
Hi community!

I wanted to ask about the differences of data conversion in the target and in the source system.

My questions would be specific to your reasoning of why you choose one approach and if you need to do both. As I understand it, some reasons are the new features on a target system and the limitation of data on the source system.

Also, the general approaches involve, as it is stated, that the main conversions and data cleansing happen on the named systems. Only small adjustments would be made on the other system to make the data transfer possible in the first place.

I would appreciate any information on this topic.

Best regards and much thanks!

entry.title: How to create SAC story on top of on-premise SAP GRC system data
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

My client wants to create SAC report on top of on-premise SAP GRC system data. From architecture point of view, what will be the best approach to bring SAP GRC data to SAP Analytics Cloud story?

We can think of few different architectures. But not sure which one will be best possible way and recommended by SAP. Please suggest –

  1. Creating calculation views on HANA data base of SAP GRC system and access the calculation view from SAC ( Can be a security issue as I will be accessing system database directly)
  2. Or if we have embedded analytics available in SAP GRC, we can create ABAP CDS view query which can be consumed through SAC. (But I am not sure if Embedded analytics module is available in SAP GRC system or not)
  3. Or do we need to extract SAP GRC data to SAP BW system first and then create BW queries to consume in SAC. (But I am not sure if there will be security issue while extracting data from GRC system to BW system)

Please suggest. Thanks in Advance.

Regards,

Animikh

entry.title: SuccessFactors Data Model and Database structure
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am looking for an information what is precisely the structure of data tables lying underneath of XML based foundation objects in SF on the one side, and MDF based foundation objects on the other side. 

In my understanding, XML based objects have three-like data structure with one to one relation and pretty rigid forms, as for MDF based objects the data structure is relational and more flexible (rows, columns). It would be important for me to understand potential impact in a positive and negative sense. It goes even further if i think on how HANA data base is structured and how SF on hana can use (if at all) the way hana is (or supposed to be) structured (column,  indexing etc.). 

Can somebody please help me out regarding and documentation out there (I could not find nothing in SAP Help Portal or KBAs)? Thanks!

Best regards,

Milos

entry.title: Report Story Widget Showing Incorrect Total because of Blank Row
entry.content:
Hi Everyone!

I have a report story that has Comp Planning and Variable pay. The widgets count associate ID's with what type of bonus payouts. When proxying as a manager running it shows an extra blank line (all dashes) assuming its their own data but its throwing the totals off of my widgets to show 1 person which is not correct. It should see zero I don't know how to remove it because as a superadmin it doesn't appear for me to remove it as I see everyone.

entry.title: GL Accounts Balance App
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I hope everyone is doing well.

I kindly request you help and advice for solving the business challenge that I have.

Let me explain my requirements, I need to deploy a FIORI application with the GL Balances with filtering on Ledger, GL account, Period, Fiscal Year and an interval of Posting dates and the following fields has to be displayed:

  • Ledger
  • GL account
  • GL account description
  • Initial Value (Balance for the previous period)
  • Debit for the period
  • Credit for the period
  • Balance for the period
  • Initial value + Balance for the period 

To try to achieve this I have looked at the analytics application from SAP "Journal Entry Analyzer", didn't help because I can't get the previous period balance. After this I searched about the VDM and tried to work around with the tiles "Custom Analytical Queries" and "Custom CDS Views" to enhance the CDS C_GLLineItemsQ0001 and I_GLAcctBalanceCube but didn't work either. Now I'm thinking of building custom CDS with FIORI elements to able to achieve the requirements.

Could you give me some advice how to better achieve this? Or maybe a different approach that could help? 

Thank you,

Best Regards,

João Vieira

entry.title: How to rename custom analytical application created via View-Browser
entry.content:
Hello how can an analytical application, which was created via custom analytical query and the App View-Browser, be renamed? It doesn't show the given description but the technical name of the query. How can the highlighted name be changed?
Thanks and best regards

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Data Analyzer
entry.content:
Hi,
Can we modify the Builder Panel of the Data Analyzer, as if I want only selected Measures/Dimensions when i open Data Analyzer through URL Parameter.

entry.title: Error Update statistics of table failed in ATM Logs (ASE db)
entry.content:
Hello,

I get the following error message for different tables in my ATM logs in the DBACOCKPIT: Update statistics of table failed

The tables are both in saptools and in the area in which the data from the tables in SAP are located. The db is an Sybase ASE db (version: 16.0.04.04). The SAP version is 753 

I couldn't find any noticeable consequences but would like to resolve these errors. Does anyone know why this happens and how I can solve it?

I could only find solutions for this error if the update is executed manually.

Thank you

entry.title: system tables of DSP aren't visible in core DSP
entry.content:
Hi, 

 

Is there a way to access system tables like VIEW_COLUMNS in core SAP Data Sphere (excluding Hana Explorer)?

 

 

Thanks

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Configuration to Import Data from API
entry.content:
Hello experts,
I am trying to import data from an API into sap analytics cloud, but it displays an error message when connecting.
I made the same connection through postman and it worked, but the SAC displays an error message.
More information: 
Connection type: Odata Services
Authentication Type: OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials

On system monitor shows the message:

 {"packageId":"sap.fpa.services.resource.dataAcquisition","objectName":"message_dataAcquisition","messageId":"AUTHENTICATION_ERROR","args":["Unauthorized"],"message":"Unable to connect to datasource. Check the connection details and user credentials, and then try again.","previous":null,"bUIMessage":false,"bWarning":false,"customData":"Authentication Failed:PKIX path building failed: sun.security.provider.certpath.SunCertPathBuilderException: unable to find valid certification path to requested target (If this is using a shared connection without credentials, please enter the credentials by running a data import using such connection)","status":401}
what could be causing the error?

Thanks,
Lo Bello

entry.title: Step by step guide for creating KPI in SAP C4C Stories / Embedded SAC
entry.content:
Creating Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) in SAP Cloud for Customer (C4C) Stories/ Embedded SAC  can be a difficult task, especially when a standard template is not provided by SAP. This step-by-step guide aims to simplify the process, providing clear and concise instructions to help you set up KPIs in C4C Stories / Embedded SAC functionality. Let’s dive in and transform your data into meaningful metrics!

Step 1: Create a new story, drag and drop data source in query designer, select relevant fields and select correct Access Context.



Step 2: Create a calculated column which would act as KPI Target. Make sure to change Aggregation to MAX.



Step 3: Insert a comparison chart in story with Expected Value and Test KPI Target as measure. Display Test KPI Target as triangle in this chart.

 

Step 4: Add reference line to display Target in comparison chart.

Step 5: Add variance to comparsion chart to calculate percentage and absolute difference between Expected Value and Test Target KPI.

Following steps are optional and in following step I am creating the same KPI but showing percentage and absolute value difference in different way.

Step 6: Create a calculated measure (addition of Expected Value and Test KPI Target) in comparison chart. This calculated measure will be used in variance hence dont display this calculated measure on screen.

Step 7: Use this calculated measure in variance to display percentage and absolute difference in positive value rather than negative.

In the image above, Test KPI v1 indicates the additional sales needed to reach the target, while Test KPI v2 displays the sales that have already been achieved.

Thank you for taking the time to read this guide on creating KPIs in SAP C4C Stories. I hope you found it helpful and informative. If you have any questions, need further assistance, or would like to share your feedback, please feel free to reach out to me. You can connect with me on: https://www.linkedin.com/in/chenthala-vishnu-srinivasullu-0014ba135

Apologies for typo and grammar mistakes.

 

Apologies for typo and grammar mistakes.😉

entry.title: Fiscal Period input parameter in custom analytical query default to current period
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a requirement to build a custom analytical query using FMBDP and FMIFIIT tables. Both has the data maintained based on Fiscal period wise and there is a requirement to display the Budget and FI value for the current month / current period other than sum of from Period 001 to Period 016.

The custom analytical query will have an input of Fiscal period /Current Period with the current period value as default. 

For ex: If the user runs the report for this month, then the Fiscal/Current period input has 009 as default. 

How this can be achieved in the custom analytical query? 

if we cannot make it in the Custom analytical query and it can be achieved by CDS view as a column/field using the system date or date function then please advise how it can be determined or write the logic for the same.

Thanks.

Best Regards.

entry.title: Catalog Object Grant ACCESS Issue in HANA DB
entry.content:
Hello All,

Could you please help with your inputs on the below case.

We have one HANA Stored Procedure. This is a directly created as a Catalog object by some Developer & that got migrated to Production as well.  He is the Owner of that Procedure , as its created by his ID.

Now that Developer has left the Organization , we want to grant that Procedure ( Catalog object) with EXECUTE privilege to some Technical ID which needs to call that procedure from external system ( ex: Informatica).

How can we assign that access to that technical ID , as the Developer is no longer available? 

In this case SYSTEM user also will not have access to grant access to other except the Owner.

Br

Kiran K

entry.title: KPI-Tile missing category after refresh
entry.content:
Hello,

I have created a KPI-tile for the Fiori-Launchpad that is based on a custom CDS-View.

I created an ODATA-Service for my view and also tested it in the SAP Gateway Client.
The data is shown as expected.

My problem is that the values in my KPI-tile are not working as I intended.
I have following values that I get from the CDS-View (these are the Values I want to show in my KPI-tile):


And when I log into the Fiori-Launchpad the tile looks how I wanted it to look:


But when I refresh the tile it looks like this:


When I refresh the whole website it shows the right values again.
But indepentend of the tile, the drilldown Report behind the KPI-tile always shows the wrong values:

My first thought was that I configured something wrong, but to me everything looks right:

I want it to look something like this, right now only one category is shown:

Has anyone experienced this, or can help me to solve it?

Lars

entry.title: Scheduling Stories - Dynamic Time Filters Issue
entry.content:
We would like to be able to use dynamic time filters in Stories for running and scheduling reports - but they are not in line with currently available scheduling filters.

Filters like "Previous Month" work fine when we run the reports, but they fail when we schedule them, because the As of Date options for Time Filters in scheduling allow only for a calendar date - so when we schedule a report as of August 1st, the Previous Month dynamic filter always shows July, no matter whether the schedule runs in August or September.

Will it be possible to schedule Stories with dynamic as of date filter? At least to schedule them as of Today?

Aga Czoska, Kennametal
